D8 AnthropologyL3 ApplyReference

Vermilion / Cinnabar — Mercury Sulfide Red

α-HgS (cinnabar) is a bandgap semiconductor; bandgap 2.0 eV absorbs blue-green, reflecting saturated red. Almadén Spain produced it for 2,000 years

α-HgS has a direct bandgap near 2.0 eV — absorbs everything shorter than ~620 nm, reflects red. Ground from cinnabar ore or made by subliming Hg + S at 580 °C ("Chinese vermilion"). Toxicity halted production: Roman slaves mining Almadén died young; modern paints use synthetic cadmium red (CdSe) instead.

Slide grain size — coarser grains scatter more red light → lighter, finer grains → richer crimson.
